raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "performative self-affirmation and intention-setting that enacts a shift toward relational boundaries and mutuality",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "mutuality is attainable and desirable across all relational contexts",
    "the speaker previously used overgiving as a safety strategy",
    "collapse and overgiving are controllable choices rather than automatic patterns",
    "co-regulation in truth is a stable, preferable alternative to overgiving",
    "others are capable of and willing to meet the speaker as they are"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent; each clause reinforces the others around the theme of non-collapsing presence and reciprocal relating, with no direct contradictions",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the speaker possesses sufficient self-regulation capacity, the statement accurately maps their developmental edge, and repetition supports measurable behavioral change without bypassing real relational asymmetries",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the statement functions as a defensive idealization that masks ongoing collapse, overgiving, or inability to tolerate others' failure to meet them, or when applied rigidly to unsafe or exploitative relationships"
}
